title: Clean the missing charges
prompt:
A churn DataFrame **`df`** is preloaded (`pandas` as `pd`, `numpy` as `np` available). Columns: `customer_id`, `tenure_months`, `monthly_charge`, `plan`, `support_calls`, `contract`, `churned`.

`monthly_charge` has missing values. Impute them with the **median** of the column, in place in `df`, and define:

- `charge_median` — the median you used (float)
- `clean_mean` — mean of `monthly_charge` **after** imputation, rounded to 2 decimals

Use **Run** + `print(...)` to explore first. Why median and not mean here? (Be ready to defend it.)
starter_code:
# df is preloaded — explore with print(df.head()), print(df.isna().sum())

charge_median = ...
clean_mean = ...
hint_notes: Median is robust to the right-skewed charges. Tags: mean-vs-median-imputation, no-missingness-check.
